title: ai — authorization policies
description: Three policies covering the three owned entities. Persona-role authorization is enforced by AuthorizePersonaRole HTTP middleware (reads `#[RequiresRoles]`) BEFORE the run controller — policies here handle post-run access to the persisted rows. Every run + tool-call re-checks RBAC inside the vendor agent pipeline via ToolAuthorizerDecorator — the LLM's promise is not authorization.

abilities.scope: conversation user sees own runs; admin sees tenant-wide
conversation user + admin. status must be 'running'
audit run-cost reconciliation. Prompt + response bodies never exposed
conversation user + admin. args_hash never decrypts back to args
cross-tenant tool-abuse detection. tool_name + status only
user sees drafts they authored; admin sees tenant-wide
authoring user (self-confirm allowed for interactive drafts) + admin. Status must be 'open' + expires_at in the future
